response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior individual contributor with deep, varied engineering experience and genuine developer-tooling credentials (test frameworks, test runners, CI/CD infrastructure, developer training), but this is a people-management role requiring at least 2 years of management and 3 years of technical leadership, and the resume shows no evidence of managing people, running one-on-ones, owning team roadmaps, or leading organizational change. Mobile exposure is limited to a brief iOS/Apple TV stint a decade ago, and the current employment gap since October 2025 is unexplained. He may be an excellent IC hire, but he does not fit this manager profile.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems, well above the 8-year minimum
Authentic developer-tools and testing background: built test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), CI/CD and GitLab infrastructure, device orchestration tooling, and MCP code generators
Strong quality-engineering culture fit: TDD, trunk-based development, code katas, and delivering multi-day testing/Selenium courses
concerns: No people-management experience: no direct reports, performance management, or one-on-one responsibilities anywhere in the work history
No demonstrated strategic roadmap ownership or organizational-change leadership at team or org level
Unexplained career gap from October 2025 to present, and only shallow, dated mobile-platform experience (2015-2016)

key_factors.factor: Developer tooling and test infrastructure depth
